labheyyāhaṁ.meaning: may I receive; might I obtain
breakdown: May I receive (1st person singular optative of labhati "to gain").
I. (The two fuse: labheyyaṁ + ahaṁ → labheyyāhaṁ.)

laddhā.gram: past participle of labhati (feminine/plural form); also used as gerund "having obtained"
breakdown: Past participle suffix (labh + ta → laddha by assimilation).
Root: to be ashamed, to be modest (lajjati).

lajjī/lajjā.gram: feminine noun (lajjā) and its possessive adjective (lajjī)

lapitaṁ.gram: past participle of lapati (neuter nominative/accusative singular)

lekhasuttaṁ.meaning: the Lekhasutta, the Discourse on Inscriptions (writing)
lekhasuttaṁ.gram: proper noun, neuter (title of a sutta)

leḍḍunā.meaning: with a clod of earth; with a stone

breakdown: Root: to be greedy, to covet (lubbhati); strengthened to lobh-.
lobho.goenka: Lobha, greed or craving, is one of the three roots of unwholesome action together with dosa (aversion) and moha (delusion). Every kamma rooted in lobha multiplies one's misery. In vipassanā practice the meditator observes how the mind reacts with greed to pleasant sensations, and by observing those sensations with equanimity, understanding their impermanence, gradually weakens and eradicates this root.

lokajeṭṭhaṁ.meaning: the foremost in the world; the eldest, highest of the world

lokakkhāyikaṁ.meaning: talk about the world; speculative narration about the world
lokakkhāyikaṁ.summary: One item in the stock list of frivolous kinds of talk avoided by the meditator.
breakdown: Narration, story-telling. (The doubling kkh is junction doubling.)

lokavidū.meaning: knower of the worlds; wise about the world
lokavidū.summary: One of the nine standard epithets of the Buddha in the iti'pi so formula.
lokavidū.goenka: Lokavidū, knower of the worlds, is one of the qualities of the Buddha recollected in the iti'pi so formula chanted in this tradition. Having explored the entire field of mind and matter within the framework of his own body, the Buddha knew by direct experience all the worlds — the entire range of conditioned existence — and what lies beyond it.

lokāmisūpādānā.meaning: clinging to worldly gain; attachment to the bait of the world
breakdown: Attachment, clinging. (The vowels fuse: āmisa + upādāna → āmisūpādāna.)

lokānukampāya.meaning: out of compassion for the world
lokānukampāya.summary: Literally "trembling along with" — having pity for the suffering in the world; said of the Buddha's motivation in teaching.
breakdown: Compassion; literally "trembling along with", being moved by the suffering of others.
Dative singular ending: "for the sake of".
lokānukampāya.goenka: The lexicon highlights the literal sense: anukampā is "trembling along with", that is, having compassion, out of pity for the suffering in the world. The phrase lokānukampāya recurs in the suttas to describe why the Buddha and his disciples teach Dhamma: for the benefit and happiness of many, out of compassion for the world.

lomahaṁso.meaning: horripilation; the hair of the body standing on end

luddho.gram: past participle of lubbhati (masculine nominative singular)
breakdown: Root: to be greedy, to covet.
Past participle suffix (lubh + ta → luddha by assimilation).

luddo.gram: adjective, also used as a masculine noun (nominative singular)
breakdown: Fierce, cruel (related to Sanskrit raudra); as a noun, a hunter.

lujjati.meaning: to break; to fall apart, crumble
breakdown: Root: to break up, to dissolve.

luto.gram: past participle of lunāti "to cut" (masculine nominative singular)

lābhasakkārasilokasannissitassa.meaning: of one based upon gain, honour and fame

lābhasakkāraṁ.meaning: gain and honour; receiving and hospitality

lābhena.meaning: through gain; by means of gain

lābhena lābhaṁ.meaning: gain upon gain; (pursuing) one gain by means of another
lābhena lābhaṁ.summary: A stock phrase for insatiable acquisitiveness: chasing gain through gain.

lābhā.meaning: gain; possession (also in the idiom "it is a gain for...")
lābhā.gram: masculine noun (nominative plural or ablative singular of lābha)
breakdown: Root: to gain, obtain (labhati); strengthened to lābh-.

lābhādikāmatāya.meaning: through desire for gain and the rest (gain, offerings and renown)
lābhādikāmatāya.summary: The phrase probably refers to the standard set lābha-sakkāra-siloka: gain, offerings and renown.

lāmakācāro.meaning: of vile practice; one whose conduct is inferior

breakdown: Root: to die (marati); macca is "one destined to die", a mortal.

macchamaṁsavatthusmiṁ.meaning: with regard to fish and meat

breakdown: Noun suffix: maccu, death; also personified as Māra, the lord of death.

maccudheyya.meaning: the realm of death; the sphere of mortality

maccāna.gram: masculine noun (genitive plural of macca, shortened in verse)

madaniyaṭṭhena.meaning: on account of being intoxicating; in the sense of causing intoxication
breakdown: On account of, in the sense of (instrumental of attha; with junction doubling).

madappamādahetuto.meaning: by reason of intoxication and heedlessness
breakdown: Heedlessness, negligence. (The doubling pp is junction doubling.)

madhurattāya.meaning: for sweetness; on account of its sweetness
madhurattāya.gram: neuter abstract noun (dative form of madhuratta)

madā.gram: masculine noun (nominative plural of mada, from majjati "to be intoxicated")

maggasaccaniddeso.meaning: the Exposition of the Truth of the Path
maggasaccaniddeso.summary: Section title in the Mahāsatipaṭṭhānasutta: the detailed exposition of the Noble Eightfold Path as the truth of the way out of suffering.
maggasaccaniddeso.gram: masculine noun, compound (nominative singular; section title)

maggavaggo, dhammapadapāḷi..meaning: the Maggavagga (Chapter on the Path) of the Dhammapada
breakdown: The Dhammapada text of the Pāli Canon.

maggenamaggappaṭipattiadhivāsananti.meaning: assent to practising the path by the path; mental consent to engaging (in sexual intercourse)
maggenamaggappaṭipattiadhivāsananti.summary: A difficult technical term: it refers to actually permitting and engaging in sexual intercourse, pointing to the intentional concomitant of mental agreement.
breakdown: Path. (The doubling pp follows in junction with paṭipatti.)
Quotation marker; the niggahīta ṁ + ti becomes nti.

mahaggata.summary: Used of the mind in meditation: cittaṁ mahaggataṁ, the expanded mind, as observed in cittānupassanā.
breakdown: Gone, become (past participle of gacchati); mahaggata is literally "gone to greatness".

mahantā.gram: adjective (plural or feminine form of mahanta)

breakdown: Fruit, result. (The doubling pph is junction doubling.)

mahapphalāni great.gram: adjective, compound, neuter plural (duplicate row; see mahapphalāni)
mahapphalāni great.note: uncertain: this headword is a glossary-row artifact in which the English gloss "great" was attached to the headword mahapphalāni; see the entry mahapphalāni.
